title: Tuknatan
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Character |type=Jedi |image=[[File:Tuknatan.jpg]] |name=Tuknatan |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 3]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=Brown |feathers= |eyes=Blue |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|The Force is with us!|Tuknatan during the Battle of Coruscant|}} '''Tuknatan''' was a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] active during the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]. He was present at a [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] session in [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], where fellow [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Master [[Memit Nadill]] and [[Empress/Legends|Empress]] [[Teta/Legends|Teta]] of the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Koros system]] warned of the impending return of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. Nadill and Teta's forebodings came true when the Sith [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Hyperspace War)|attacked]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] with a seemingly unstoppable invasion force. Tuknatan, Nadill, and a group of other Jedi fought valiantly in the battle, but it seemed that they had no chance of victory. However, a large portion of the Sith force suddenly disappeared, revealing that they were mere [[Mind trick/Legends|illusions]]. Taking advantage of the opportunity, Tuknatan led a charge into the heart of the Sith force, and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] won the Battle of Coruscant. ==Biography== {{Quote|For a hundred generations the Jedi have fought to protect the Republic!''"
"''We're not gonna stop now!|A Jedi and Tuknatan, during the Battle of Coruscant|}} Tuknatan, a [[Gender/Legends|male]] member of a brown-furred [[Species/Legends|species]], was a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] active in the years leading up to the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]. In [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], [[Draethos/Legends|Draethos]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]] saw a [[Force vision/Legends|vision]] of the return of the long-lost [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] and reported it to Jedi Master [[Memit Nadill]] and [[Empress/Legends|Empress]] [[Teta/Legends|Teta]] of the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Koros system]]. Alarmed, Nadill, Odan-Urr, and Teta ventured to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in order to inform the rest of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] in the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]]. Tuknatan, along with several other members of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], attended this Senate session. Although many of the present Jedi dismissed the forebodings, Tuknatan remained silent on the subject.[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 4]] Odan-Urr's vision proved to be true when the Sith emerged and [[Battle of Koros Major (Great Hyperspace War)|attacked]] the [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Empress Teta/Legends|Koros Major]] just a short time later. Upon the outbreak of the Battle of Koros Major, Memit Nadill flew to Coruscant to warn the Jedi there; Tuknatan was among several Jedi who greeted him at his landing pad. Despite Nadill's warning, however, they were unable to prepare, as just moments after Nadill's arrival Coruscant's invasion alarms sounded, signaling the arrival of the Sith. In the Coruscant skies, a large Sith [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], consisting of tens of thousands of [[Warship/Legends|warships]], appeared out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. The warships deployed [[Dropship/Legends|dropships]] to the surface, containing Sith [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] warriors and [[Sith war behemoth]]s. Tuknatan and the other Jedi decided to make a [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Hyperspace War)|stand]] on the steps of the [[Senate Building/Legends|Senate Building]]. Tuknatan and the Republic forces leaped headlong into the fury of the battle, but the Sith force seemed unstoppable and the chances of victory appeared slim. Suddenly, however, a large portion of the Sith force simply disappeared; they were mere [[Mind trick/Legends|illusions]]. [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]], the commander of the Sith invasion force and the creator of the illusions, had had his concentration broken courtesy of a shot on his [[Sith Meditation Sphere]] by his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]] [[Gav Daragon]], causing his illusions to fade away into nothingness. Emboldened by this turn of events, Tuknatan and the other Jedi charged into the heart of the Sith invasion and fought ferociously, and the Battle of Coruscant was won. Overcome by the elation of the victory, Tuknatan and the Jedi exclaimed that the Republic would never fall.[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 4]] ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|It is time for a counterstrike with every weapon that remains!|Tuknatan|}} Tuknatan was a brave and valiant Jedi, one who showed no hesitation in standing and fighting against the Sith, even when victory seemed an unlikely prospect. Tuknatan was ferocious and aggressive while fighting, recommending an all-out counterstrike against the Sith during the Battle of Coruscant. He was a fierce supporter of the Galactic Republic and believed that the Republic would never fall. In battle, rather than using a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] like most other Jedi, Tuknatan simply wielded a gold-colored staff. ==Behind the scenes== The character of Tuknatan first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith]]'', a story arc in the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]]. He appeared briefly in the arc's fourth issue, and reappeared in ''The Golden Age of the Sith''{{'s}} follow-up arc, [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire|''Tales of the Jedi: The Fall of the Sith Empire'']], in which he had a more substantial role. In these appearances, Tuknatan was drawn by [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]], and preliminary black-and-white sketches of Tuknatan appear in the back of ''Golden Age of the Sith''{{'s}} first issue.[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 0|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 0]] ==Appearances== *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 4]] {{1st}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 3]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 4]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 5]] ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Tuknatan}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Tuknatan |pl=Tuknatan }} [[Category:Individuals of unidentified species]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Pre-Ruusan Jedi Masters]]
